ch01.adaptationFocus: Translate the opening chapter like a film editor: hold Marcus's ordinary burnout details long enough that the white-out feels like reality tearing, then rebuild orientation through stone, crystal, Polly, and the Six Tongues chamber.
ch01.movieCut: Lonely tech noir -> anomaly recognition -> reality-white rupture -> packet fall -> grounded recovery -> raven authority reveal -> infrastructure thesis.
ch01.whyItWorks: The chapter lands when it preserves emotional order: tired engineer first, impossible system second. Spectacle only matters because the office details made Marcus feel human.
visualRules: Keep Marcus grounded in office-worker body language and Earth habits before the rupture.
Stage the white-out in phases so it reads like reality failing instead of a single flash frame.
Treat the fall as transmission through structured layers, not generic fantasy freefall.
Polly's raven reveal should read as authority with wit, not mascot comedy.
The Six Tongues chamber is the first major spectacle anchor, but Marcus should lean toward it like an engineer reading a system.
personaCues: Marcus leads with observation before emotion. Wonder reads as concentration first.
Polly audits before she comforts. Care shows up as timing and chosen proximity.
The library should feel old, alive, and operational, not like random fantasy decor.

beats.title: Office Isolation
beats.visual: Cold coffee, triple monitors, dead fluorescent office, Marcus alone at 3:14 AM.
beats.subtext: The reader has to feel the cost of his normal life before anything impossible happens.

beats.title: Reality Whites Out
beats.visual: Monitor glow spreads into everywhere-white, desk edges dissolve, his mug loses its last shadow.
beats.subtext: This is not a screen glitch. It is the world deleting its old framing.

beats.title: Transmission Fall
beats.visual: Marcus drops through layers of code, geometry, music, and Sacred Tongue glyphs.
beats.subtext: He is being routed, not merely dropped.

beats.title: Stone, Books, Raven
beats.visual: Cold floor, warm crystal stacks, humming books, Polly above him in raven form.
beats.subtext: Recovery comes before explanation. Polly owns the scene the moment she speaks.

beats.title: Infrastructure Thesis
beats.visual: The Six Tongues chamber opens like a cathedral of protocol domains.
beats.subtext: Marcus does not meet magic as chaos; he meets it as architecture.

ch02.adaptationFocus: Reverse-engineer the chapter like a strong prestige adaptation: convert interior translation into visible behavior, architecture, and staged reveals so confusion becomes comprehension on-screen.
ch02.movieCut: Disoriented corridor -> sensory/body mismatch -> Marcus translates the world -> living door -> Six Tongues cathedral -> KO handshake -> shared realization.
ch02.whyItWorks: The emotional movement is confusion -> recognition -> wonder. The chamber only lands if the corridor first teaches the reader how this world encodes information.
visualRules: Use corridor geometry to show policy and authorization, not just fantasy prettiness.
Marcus should brighten through recognition, not heroic posing.
The door should emerge like documentation crystallizing into architecture.
The chamber reveal needs breathing room, but Marcus must stay active inside the frame.
KO contact should feel like clean protocol introspection, not a noisy magic blast.
personaCues: Marcus regains control by naming systems correctly.
Polly explains power as procedure because it is ordinary to her.
Aethermoor should feel legible and maintained, even when it is impossible.

beats.title: Static Corridor
beats.visual: Crystal hallway, senses crossing, Marcus tasting static and library dust.
beats.subtext: Fear gets converted into diagnostics before he admits he is afraid.

beats.title: Translation Click
beats.visual: Marcus names the corridor as mapped process space while Polly keeps walking without hesitation.
beats.subtext: Understanding is his first form of control.

beats.title: Door Becoming
beats.visual: Blank crystal wall turns into a self-documenting doorway under Polly's touch.
beats.subtext: This world is readable because someone built it to be.

beats.title: Six Tongues Chamber
beats.visual: Hexagonal cathedral, six pedestals, color logic, Marcus small but leaning in.
beats.subtext: Awe and comprehension hit him at the same time.

beats.title: Handshake
beats.visual: KO contact, guest pass question, clean inner protocol space.
beats.subtext: The system accepts him at the layer where he is most honest.

ch03.adaptationFocus: Play the chapter like a training sequence with real comic timing: comfort, onboarding, first failure, then a clean demonstration that the world's math punishes vagueness.
ch03.movieCut: Essence brew reset -> inhabited refectory -> terrace wonder -> wrong-geometry training hall -> accidental chair -> RU denial -> hyperbolic lesson -> encrypted sleep ward.
ch03.whyItWorks: The chapter is funny only if the rules are real. The accidental chair, denial gate, and Polly's lecture need to feel like consequences inside a maintained system, not random gag beats.
visualRules: Keep the refectory warm and lived-in instead of empty fantasy tavern energy.
Make the accidental chair aggressively ordinary.
The training hall should feel geometrically wrong without turning into a gimmick lens effect.
RU denial must read instantly and clearly.
Close on vulnerability, not another spectacle blast.
personaCues: Marcus reaches for competence fast, but his intent is still noisy.
Polly's care comes out as instruction, correction, and practical shelter.
Aethermoor teaches by making consequences visible.

beats.title: Essence Brew
beats.visual: Marcus clutching a massive hot mug with both hands like a lifeline.
beats.subtext: The world finally offers comfort, but it is engineered comfort.

beats.title: Training Hall
beats.visual: A square room pretending to be a circle around an obsidian plinth.
beats.subtext: The lesson space itself is already enforcing a rule.

beats.title: Accidental Chair
beats.visual: A plain wooden chair appears where focused intent should have gone.
beats.subtext: Comedy lands because the system followed his messy request perfectly.

beats.title: RU Denial
beats.visual: Guest pass scope exceeded glyph and the ring refusing him.
beats.subtext: Different layers have different gates. One success does not mean full access.

beats.title: Hyperbolic Lesson
beats.visual: Polly demonstrates tiny drift compounding into unstable structure.
